title: Odissi elegance casts a spell at Koodalmanikyam festival
content: Madhulita Mohapatra and Nrityantar Ensemble weave devotion and artistry into a captivating performance

title: Kerala sees a surge in ‘NEET-friendly’ clothing as dress code rules tighten
content: With strict rules banning metal fastenings, layered clothing and accessories, NEET dress codes are driving students to seek compliant outfits — spurring a niche market for custom, exam-ready, seasonal clothing in Kerala

title: Bowring tragedy: CM orders suspension of official, Lokayukta takes up inquiry, police file UDR
content: Inputs have also been requested from the IMD regarding rainfall intensity in the area at the time of the incident

title: Bengaluru’s Bowring Hospital wall collapse leaves a trail of loss, pain, and grief
content: Seven persons were killed and seven others were injured after a portion of the compound wall of Bowring and Lady Curzon Hospital collapsed during heavy rain in Bengaluru.

title: Bihar trapped in a vicious cycle of unchecked bureaucracy, corruption: Tejashwi Yadav
content: Raising questions over the delay in the expansion of the Bihar Cabinet, the RJD leader pointed out that it has been over a fortnight, but only three individuals are running the government and taking arbitrary decisions

title: Citing Kharge’s remarks, Nikhil Kumaraswamy slams Congress over ‘high command’ culture
content: Trends shown by exit polls are encouraging for the BJP-JD(S) combine ahead of the 2028 Assembly polls, according the youth wing president

title: ECI clears ‘Telangana Rakshana Sena’ as Kavitha’s party name for publication
content: The final approval of the party name depends on the objections received to public notice published in newspapers

title: SPMVV to conduct LAWCET and PGLCET 2026 on  May 4
content: A total of 30,020 candidates, including 20,101 male and 9,919 female candidates, have registered for the test, says SPMVV Vice-Chancellor

title: Cabinet approves funds for infrastructure works at Amaravati
content: A sum of ₹2,540 crore sanctioned for works in the integrated Secretariat and Heads of Departments complex, ₹1,208 crore for construction of twin office towers in Quantum Valley and ₹798 crore for Assembly building

title: Assembly election 2026 LIVE: Exit polls aired at BJP's instructions to demoralise TMC workers, says CM Mamata
content: Election Commission is looking into repolling demands in 77 booths across four Assembly constituencies in West Bengal's South 24 Parganas district

title: Former Air Chief says Greater Nicobar project important for ‘military footprint’
content: In pushback against Rahul Gandhi’s opposition to the project, retired Air Chief Marshal says ecological concerns will be mitigated; notes that proposed port is 150 km from Malacca Strait, the route for 75% of China’s energy trade

title: Efforts on to handover Shivamogga Airport to AAI, says MP B.Y. Raghavendra
content: Shivamogga MP B.Y. Raghavendra says that on his request to hand over the airport to AAI, the State government sent an IAS officer to Delhi for talks.

title: Titan Group keen on investing in defence manufacturing, MRO facilities in Andhra Pradesh
content: In a meeting with Infrastructure and Investment Minister Janardhan Reddy, the company representatives highlight the extensive experience they have across sectors such as aviation, aerospace, defence, space, mining, and trading

title: Judicial probe sought into death of student Aditya
content: Deputy Leader of Opposition in the Assembly Arvind Bellad has said the student was forced to take the extreme step following police harassment

title: No ‘immediate plans’ on change of guard in Karnataka, says Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge
content: ‘Some may say I should become the Chief Minister. You may say so, others may say so, and even the high command may think so. In our party, most of the decisions are taken by Sonia Gandhi’

title: Palle Veekshana to be held across 700 villages in Nellore district, says Collector
content: Himanshu Shukla tours Ananthavaram, inspects employment guarantee works and makes suggestions to the officials regarding the quality of the works

title: Rajnath Singh asks Italy to avoid defence tech sharing with Pakistan
content: Talks with Italian Defence Minister focus on exclusive technologies, India-Italy cooperation and security concerns

title: Special schools in A.P. record 100% pass rate in SSC exams
content: A total of 54 students of special schools appeared for the examinations this year and all of them passed

title: Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu calls for restructuring of courses offered by universities in Andhra Pradesh
content: Emphasising the need for ‘course forecasting’ in line with the emerging trends, the Chief Minister lauds Andhra University for launching a course in quantum technology, whose adoption is high on government agenda

title: Property tax rebate to continue till May 31
content: The Hubballi-Dharwad Municipal Corporation has decided to extend the 5% rebate on payment of property tax

title: FIR filed against hospital superintendent and doctors in child’s rabies death
content: Niya Faisal was bitten by a stray dog on April 8, 2025, and despite initial treatment at the hospital, her condition worsened, leading to her death on May 5 at a specialised facility in Thiruvananthapuram.

title: Watch: I sense clear majority for DMK in Tamil Nadu: Mallikarjun Kharge
content:
AICC President Mallikarjun Kharge on Thursday expressed confidence that the alliances of which the Congress is a part, will get a clear majority in Tamil Nadu and Kerala, where Assembly elections were held earlier this month.
In Assam, where Assembly polls were held on April 9, Congress will win more seats than the exit polls show, he said. He added that the grand old party gave a tough fight in Puducherry, and in West Bengal the fight has been for the party’s growth.

title: Anantapur officials asked to prepare drinking water supply plans for May, June
content: Finance Minister says that if drinking water issue arises anywhere, it must be resolved within 24 hours or else the official concerned will be issued a charge memo

title: Rain brings respite to Kerala; more showers ahead
content: The temperature, which had crossed the 40°C mark many times in April, fell close to the normal range in large parts of the State; yellow alert issued for four districts on Friday

title: Andhra Pradesh Cabinet approves enhanced benefits for Polavaram-displaced families
content: It decides to raise the unit cost for house construction from ₹2.85 lakh to ₹3.57 lakh; revised estimate of ₹309 crore for modernising Punganur branch canal, allocation of 350 acres in Puttaparthi for DRDO’s defence manufacturing unit, and ₹1,586 crore infra push for three fishing harbours also get the nod

title: Kappad Blue Flag beach to undergo ₹5-crore infrastructure overhaul
content: The initial phase will be backed by a ₹1-crore allocation and will address essential maintenance and the restoration of basic amenities. The subsequent ₹4-crore phase will focus on extensive aesthetic enhancements and site preservation, says the DTPC

title: From TACO To NACHO: Hormuz Crisis Earns Trump New, Unfortunate, Nickname
content: The nickname was coined by Bloomberg columnist Javier Blas, who said that the nickname shows how frustrated the public is about the on-and-off blockade on the Strait of Hormuz, which has disrupted oil flow and caused the gas prices to spike.

title: 17 Killed As Jeep Plunges Into 700 Metre Deep Gorge In Nepal
content: The private jeep was hired by locals travelling to attend the Baisakh Purnima festival on Friday at Jaljala, according to police inspector Sunil Thapa Nepali.

title: 2nd Term For DMK In Tamil Nadu, TVK To Do Better Than AIADMK: Today's Chanakya Exit Poll
content: Today's Chanakya has said the DMK-led alliance will win 125 seats (with a margin of 11) in the 234-member Assembly.

title: India Too Modest About Contributions: Science Museum Group Chief Ian Blatchford To NDTV
content: Blatchford, who has been travelling to India for over 25 years, said the relationship between India and the UK is entering a more substantive phase.

title: BJP Sweep In Bengal, Trinamool A Distant Second, Predicts Today's Chankya Exit Poll
content: The BJP, Today's Chanakya predicted, could get 192 seats (plus/minus 11 seats) and the Trinamool Congress 100 seats (plus/minus 11 seats). The rest could get two seats (plus/minus two seats).

title: BJP To Sweep Assam, 50 Per Cent Vote Share: Today's Chanakya Exit Poll
content: Today's Chanakya predicted that the Congress combine is projected to win 23 seats (with a margin of 9 seats) with a vote share of 38 per cent (with a margin of 3 per cent).

title: From UP's Badlands To Bhopal's Billionaires: Harry Boxer's Extortion Empire
content: The scale of the crackdown is staggering. So far, eight arrests have been made in Bhopal and Ashoknagar-related extortion cases, while another 20 arrests have been made in a parallel extortion case targeting a cotton businessman in Khargone district.

title: Clean Energy Investments More Consequential Than Many Realise: Jeffrey Sachs
content: What Adani Green pushes is extraordinarily important for this country. It is operating at a major scale, said US economist Jeffrey Sachs.

title: 'We Will Win Over 226 Seats, Don't Trust Exit Polls': Mamata Banerjee On Bengal Election
content: West Bengal assembly election 2026 exit polls: "... I have complete faith in the massive mandate given by the people," Mamata Banerjee said

title: US-Israel-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: Trump Floats New Plan Aimed At Creating Maritime Alliance To Reopen Strait Of Hormuz
content: US-Israel-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: Iran's President Masoud Pezeshkian told Pakistani PM Shehbaz Sharif during a phone call Saturday night that the US "should first remove operational obstacles, including the blockade"

title: UK PM Heckled With Shouts Of "Jew Harmer" After London Knife Attacks
content: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer was on Thursday heckled with shouts of "Jew harmer" as he visited north London to meet emergency services staff a day after knife attacks left two men in hospital.

title: Opinion: Opinion | If Mamata Banerjee Wins Again, It Might Be Only Because Of One Particular Group
content: Anyone familiar with the recent political and electoral history of West Bengal knows that while the BJP has done extremely well in north Bengal, it has so far failed to breach the Mamata citadel of south Bengal.

title: Dawood Aide Salim Dola Sent To Anti-Drugs Agency Custody Till May 8 Over Drug Case
content: A Mumbai court on Thursday remanded alleged international drug trafficker Salim Ismail Dola to the custody of the Narcotics Control Bureau (NCB) till May 8 in connection with a 2023 drug trafficking case, following his recent arrest after nearly two

title: British Jews Call On Starmer To Address Antisemitism After London Stabbing
content: He has also presented himself as a whistleblower in a corruption case that allegedly led to the imprisonment of former Pakistani Prime Minister Imran Khan.

title: India's Indus Sacrifice, Unequal Concessions And Pakistan's Aggression
content: When British India was partitioned in 1947, the Indus River System was also divided between the two successor states.

title: "Atmosphere Of Fear": Axis My India On Why It's Not Sharing Bengal Poll Data
content: "They will not tell you at all. They look at the camera people and then there will be trouble... There is such an atmosphere of fear," Pradeep Gupta said

title: Today's Chanakya exit poll: BJP to sweep Bengal in setback for Mamata; TVK big disruptor in TN
content: Today's Chanakya exit polls predict a landslide victory for the BJP in Assam, indicating a second term for Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma. In Kerala, the projections show a close contest between the incumbent LDF and the UDF, with the BJP expected to secure a few seats. Projections for West Bengal and Tamil Nadu are still awaited.

title: 4 drown after cruise boat capsizes in MP's Bargi river; 12 missing, rescue ops on
content: Tragedy struck in Jabalpur, Madhya Pradesh, as a cruise boat capsized in Bargi Dam, resulting in four fatalities. Officials confirmed that while 15 individuals have been rescued, search operations continue for 12 still missing. Rescue teams are actively working to locate all passengers following the devastating incident.

title: ‘No place in Gulf, except...: Iran’s Khamenei slams ‘Great Satan’ US, rejects nuclear curbs
content: Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei declared Iran's nuclear and missile capabilities non-negotiable, calling the US the "Great Satan" and asserting that foreigners have no place in the Persian Gulf. He emphasized that Iran's technological and military assets are national treasures to be protected by its population.

title: KPMG to lay off employees in its advisory division in America; confirming job cuts
content: KPMG has initiated layoffs impacting around 400 employees in its US Advisory division, a move stemming from a strategic restructuring due to shifting demand in consulting services. While regulatory risk, customer operations, and financial services are affected, growth areas like AI and strategy remain strong. This follows earlier workforce adjustments in the audit business.

title: 4, 4, 4, 4, 4! Virat Kohli joins elite RCB club after tearing apart Rabada | Watch
content: Virat Kohli achieved a special milestone in IPL 2026, smashing five consecutive fours off Kagiso Rabada in a single over. This rare feat places him alongside Chris Gayle and Shane Watson in Royal Challengers Bengaluru history. Despite Rabada striking back later, Kohli's explosive start set a strong tone for RCB.

title: From Honey Singh to Badshah and Nora Fatehi: Are ‘objectionable’ songs influencing youth behaviour?
content: Authorities across India are taking action against songs and content deemed vulgar and glorifying crime, leading to takedowns and FIRs. This crackdown, involving police, courts, and regulatory bodies, sparks debate on balancing creative freedom with social responsibility, particularly concerning youth influence.

title: Justice dept sues $5B tech firm for excluding Americans from applying to high-paying jobs
content: The US Department of Justice is suing tech firm Cloudera for allegedly discriminating against American job seekers. Prosecutors claim the company created a sham hiring process, favoring temporary visa holders for lucrative roles and actively deterring US workers. This lawsuit highlights the DOJ's commitment to protecting domestic employment opportunities.

title: IPL 2026: MCC defends ‘obstructing the field’ call on Angkrish Raghuvanshi
content: The Marylebone Cricket Club has defended the controversial 'obstructing the field' dismissal of KKR's Angkrish Raghuvanshi. They clarified that changing direction to run on the pitch, as Raghuvanshi did, constitutes a wilful act under the laws. The MCC emphasised that the batter's intent and actions, not whether they would have been out anyway, are key to such decisions.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO collection: Ranveer's film races towards 'Baahubali 2' global mark
content: In a remarkable cinematic journey, Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar 2' is swiftly approaching the monumental box office figures of 'Baahubali 2,' securing its spot as the top-grossing film of the year. After spending six weeks captivating audiences worldwide, it has amassed a staggering Rs 1,782.15 crore, now ranking as the third highest-grossing Indian film of all time. Read on to know more in detail.

title: Amazon’s biggest hiring of the year 2026 will be done by Connect Talent: What is it
content: Amazon is reportedly shifting its hiring strategy towards AI, with its largest recruitment drive in 2026 expected to heavily utilize AI-led interviews via its new Connect Talent platform. This system aims to automate screening, interviews, and note generation for large-scale hiring, including seasonal roles, with minimal human involvement.

title: 'They call Trump Nazi, but still attend': Erika Kirk slams scribes for 'ultimate hypocrisy'
content: Erika Kirk, CEO of Turning Point USA, criticized journalists for labeling President Trump with extreme terms while attending the White House Correspondents' Dinner. She attended to confront media over coverage of her and her late husband, Charlie Kirk, who was murdered in 2025. Kirk linked the attempted White House shooting to a climate of dehumanization fueled by media narratives.

title: Rohit, Kohli’s ‘flow does get disrupted’ as one-format players: Dhawan
content: Shikhar Dhawan believes Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli will adapt to reduced ODI frequency ahead of the 2027 World Cup due to their experience and professionalism. He noted that while less match exposure can disrupt rhythm, these seasoned players know how to manage it. Dhawan also highlighted that upcoming scheduling will provide sufficient ODI games for them to find form.

title: ‘Is he AI? Has Musk created him?’: Buttler’s hilarious Sooryavanshi remark goes viral
content: Cricket sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi continues to amaze, with England cricketer Jos Buttler jokingly questioning if the 15-year-old prodigy is 'AI'. Sooryavanshi's witty 'God-gifted' reply to 'AI chip' theories has won hearts.  Buttler lauded his fearless six-hitting against top bowlers, while Sooryavanshi rewrites IPL history, becoming the fastest to 400 runs.

title: Abhijeet on fallout with SRK: ‘His ego, my self-respect’
content: Playback singer Abhijeet Bhattacharya revisited his fallout with Shah Rukh Khan, citing the actor's ego and his own self-respect as the core reasons. He expressed hurt over the lack of an apology, feeling sidelined and denied credit, particularly after feeling his voice was used without proper acknowledgment.

title: 'TMC will get over 226 seats': Mamata rejects exit polls giving BJP edge
content: West Bengal chief minister Mamata Banerjee dismissed exit poll predictions, asserting the Trinamool Congress will secure a decisive victory.  While most surveys indicated a close contest with a slight edge for the BJP, one poll projected a strong win for the TMC.  Both parties expressed confidence in their respective wins.

title: ‘My soulmate, my best friend’: Ritika’s birthday wish for Rohit Sharma melts hearts
content: Rohit Sharma celebrated his 39th birthday with heartfelt wishes from wife Ritika Sajdeh and a joyous Mumbai Indians dressing room. Tributes poured in from the cricket fraternity, acknowledging his stellar career and leadership. Sharma's legacy as a white-ball great and successful captain continues to grow, marked by significant ICC and IPL achievements.

title: Court grants relief to Karisma Kapoor’s kids; bars Priya Kapur from assets
content: Karisma Kapoor's children and Sunjay Kapur's mother have filed legal cases against his widow, Priya Sachdev Kapur, alleging a forged will. The Delhi High Court has granted interim relief, restraining Priya from creating third-party rights over Sunjay's estate and preserving assets pending trial.

title: Mike Vrabel–Dianna Russini scandal: Athletic meeting reveals comms issues
content: The Athletic acknowledged internal communication gaps in handling the controversy involving Dianna Russini and Mike Vrabel during a brief staff meeting. While reporting continued publicly, internal messaging lacked clarity. An investigation is now underway. Russini has stepped down, while Vrabel remains supported by the New England Patriots, as the situation continues to evolve.

title: West Bengal polls: VIP treatment for 104-year-old who fought SIR & won
content: A 104-year-old voter, Sk Ibrahim, who has voted since Independence, faced anxiety when his name was initially removed from the electoral roll due to a name discrepancy. Despite missing the vote-from-home option, the East Burdwan administration facilitated his visit to the polling booth. Ibrahim, sharp and lucid, expressed happiness at voting again, questioning the initial adjudication.

title: ‘That’s not good enough’- Aakash Chopra fires warning to star RCB player
content: Aakash Chopra expresses concern over Jacob Bethell's quiet start for RCB, noting his inability to convert starts despite opportunities. He also questions Romario Shepherd's unclear role and effectiveness. For Gujarat Titans, Chopra highlights their top-order dependency and struggles to score in the death overs, posing significant challenges for the upcoming match.

title: Petrol-diesel prices going up? Govt issues clarification, here’s the truth
content: The Press Information Bureau's Fact Check unit has debunked viral claims of a significant hike in petrol and diesel prices, stating no such order has been issued. A senior government official confirmed there's no current proposal to increase retail fuel prices, reassuring consumers amidst global oil market volatility. Fuel prices in major cities remain unchanged.

title: Reliance Retail acquires Priyanka Chopra Jonas-owned hair care brand Anomaly
content: Reliance Retail has acquired Priyanka Chopra Jonas's global haircare brand Anomaly, bolstering its presence in the beauty and personal care sector. The deal grants Reliance full ownership of Anomaly's assets, with plans to expand the clean, vegan, and affordable brand through its extensive retail and digital platforms, including Tira.

title: Riyan Parag slapped with 25% fine and 1 demerit point for vaping during IPL match
content: Rajasthan Royals captain Riyan Parag has been fined 25% of his match fee and received a demerit point for breaching IPL conduct rules. The incident, captured on live broadcast, involved Parag vaping in the dressing room during a match.

title: Weather flips in Delhi-NCR: Hailstorm, rain break heatwave spell
content: Delhi-NCR experienced a sudden weather change on Thursday afternoon with a hailstorm and rain offering relief from heatwave conditions. Dark clouds gathered as the storm began around 3:30 PM, even as the city remained under a yellow alert for heatwaves. This rainfall helped moderate average temperatures, which were lower than last year's April.

title: 'I don't have hope': R Ashwin on Mumbai Indians pulling off 6-match miracle
content: Royal Challengers Bengaluru's historic IPL 2024 turnaround from 1-7 to playoffs is contrasted with Mumbai Indians' current IPL 2026 struggles. Languishing in ninth place with two wins from eight matches, the Hardik Pandya-led side faces an uphill battle.

title: Loud DJ music kills 140 chickens in UP's Sultanpur; case filed against operator
content: A poultry farm owner in Sultanpur district claims 140 chickens died after a wedding procession played loud DJ music. The incident occurred on April 25, 2026, near his farm in Dariyapur village. The owner alleges the intense noise caused panic and death among the birds. Police have filed an FIR against the DJ operator.

title: Big relief for Karisma Kapoor’s children: Delhi HC freezes Sunjay Kapur’s assets in India
content: The Delhi High Court has ordered a freeze on the estate of late industrialist Sunjay Kapur. Children of Karisma Kapoor and Sunjay Kapur filed a plea. The court has restrained the creation of third-party rights over assets. It also directed the filing of account statements. Foreign bank accounts and cryptocurrency holdings are also restricted.

title: Bengaluru rain havoc: 5,000 books worth Rs 14 lakh destroyed at iconic Church Street bookshop
content: Bengaluru experienced severe disruption as heavy rains flooded streets, metro stations, and even the Vidhana Soudha.  Thousands of books were destroyed at the iconic Bookworm store, with a significant financial loss.  Uprooted trees caused internet outages and traffic snarls, with over 170 trees falling across the city. One woman was injured by a falling tree.

title: Former BJP MLC Manoj Kumar Singh's nephew shot dead in Bihar road rage
content: In a heartbreaking episode in Siwan district, 28-year-old Harsh Kumar Singh lost his life due to a road rage altercation that left his father, Chandan Singh, in critical condition. What started as a minor accident spiraled into chaos when gunfire erupted amidst the escalating confrontation. Authorities are now in pursuit of the assailants with ongoing investigations and raid operations.

title: Chennai horror: Man hacks social media influencer wife to death, then dies by suicide
content: A domestic dispute in Adambakkam tragically ended with a husband allegedly killing his wife, a social media influencer, before taking his own life. The unemployed husband, reportedly facing financial strain, attacked his wife, who was the family's sole earner. Their younger son discovered the grim scene upon returning home. Authorities are investigating the incident.

title: 2 dead as car plunges into 150-foot-deep gorge in Himachal Pradesh
content: Two men died after their car fell into a 150-foot gorge and caught fire on the Bharwain-Hoshiarpur road in Una district. The occupants, returning from Chintpurni temple, were charred to death. In a separate incident, over a dozen Delhi devotees were injured when their mini-van rolled off the road in Bilaspur district.

title: Delhi hit-and-run: Delivery agent falls from flyover after being hit by car, dies
content: A food delivery agent tragically died after falling from the Dabri flyover in Delhi. His scooter was reportedly struck by a car, whose driver fled the scene. The victim, identified as Rajkumar, sustained severe injuries and was declared dead at a hospital. Police are investigating the incident and searching for the absconding driver.

title: Iran’s ‘secret weapon’ that could give US-Israel forces a 'heart attack'
content: Iran's Navy commander warns of a new, feared weapon soon to be unveiled, amid escalating US-Israel tensions. He claims Iranian forces conducted missile strikes on a US aircraft carrier and launched numerous reprisal attacks, while tightening control over the Strait of Hormuz. The commander also accused US forces of piracy and hostage-taking.

title: No lungi, no vote? CAPF faces backlash in Bengal as elderly voters stopped over attire
content: Central forces personnel at a polling booth in Gaighata allegedly prevented elderly men from voting in their lungis, citing dress code violations. This led to protests from villagers and Trinamool members, who accused the forces of harassment. The restriction was later withdrawn after the unrest.

title: 'Sonia Gandhi takes our decisions': Kharge on speculations over change of CM in Karnataka
content: Congress chief Mallikarjun Kharge downplayed the Karnataka chief ministerial tussle between Siddaramaiah and DK Shivakumar, stating the party will resolve the "confusion soon." This comes amid speculation fueled by a reported power-sharing agreement and a statement from Home Minister G Parameshwara suggesting Kharge could be a suitable CM.

title: Exit poll results 2026 for Bengal, Tamil Nadu, Assam, Kerala, Puducherry: Explained in 5 charts
content: Exit polls suggest a mixed verdict across states as West Bengal anticipates a hung assembly, potentially ending Mamata Banerjee's tenure. Tamil Nadu might see actor Vijay's TVK emerge as a significant force, challenging the established duopoly. Assam favors a BJP hat-trick, while Kerala's Left government faces a potential defeat. Puducherry's NDA is expected to retain power.

title: Moving beyond LPG: Which alternatives can power Indian kitchens next?
content: Translating this potential into large-scale adoption requires time, investment, and resolution of structural challenges. These include consistent feedstock aggregation, efficient processing systems, and last-mile logistics. In comparison, LPG benefits from a well-established nationwide distribution network and has played a pivotal role in expanding clean cooking access. In the current context, biogas and CBG are best positioned as complementary solutions rather than immediate substitutes for LPG.
